name: John Steinkamp and Associates
description_short: Indianapolis-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, debt collector harassment defense, and credit restoration for individuals facing financial hardship.
description_long:
John Steinkamp & Associates is a bankruptcy law practice located in Indianapolis, Indiana, operating since at least 2017 based on published case analyses. The firm positions itself as an affordable alternative for individuals struggling with overwhelming debt who need professional legal representation through the bankruptcy process. Founded by John Steinkamp, who claims thousands of cases worth of experience, the practice serves the Indianapolis metropolitan area and appears to have bilingual capacity (Spanish-language forms available on website). The firm maintains an active blog analyzing bankruptcy court decisions and their impact on Indiana residents.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy representation covering both Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy and Chapter 13 reorganization plans (3-5 year payment arrangements). Beyond filing services, they provide debt collector harassment defense under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA), credit restoration and dispute services, foreclosure defense in Indiana, and general debt relief consultation. They advertise free initial consultations, payment plans for fees, and Saturday appointment availability to accommodate working clients. Their website includes educational resources on bankruptcy myths, exemptions specific to Indiana law, credit repair post-bankruptcy, and automatic stay protections.

The firm distinguishes itself through published legal victories in debt collection lawsuits (Buckley vs. AFNI, Inc. and FDCPA cases handled by Dave Stafford), offering Saturday appointments beyond standard business hours, and providing bilingual services. They emphasize affordable fees and aggressive representation tailored to individual circumstances rather than mass processing. Their blog demonstrates ongoing engagement with changing bankruptcy law and federal court decisions affecting clients.

Honest assessment: This is a legitimate bankruptcy law practice appropriate for individuals considering Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 protection in Indiana. However, prospective clients should note that bankruptcy is a serious legal proceeding with long-term credit consequences, and the firm's claims of "thousands of cases" cannot be independently verified through the website alone. The difference between the two phone numbers listed (317-526-1462 and 317-526-1471) is unexplained. Like any legal service, quality outcomes depend on individual circumstances and the specific attorney assigned.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free initial bankruptcy consultation with no obligation
Offers flexible payment plans for legal fees, reducing upfront cost barriers
Saturday appointment availability for working individuals
Bilingual services (Spanish-language forms and 'Como le podemos ayudar' messaging available)
Published track record of favorable FDCPA and Fair Credit Reporting Act decisions
Comprehensive educational resources on Indiana-specific bankruptcy exemptions and foreclosure law
Provides debt collector harassment defense beyond just bankruptcy filing
cons: No transparent fee schedule published on website; must call for pricing information
Limited verifiable attorney credentials or Bar status information displayed publicly
Two different phone numbers listed (317-526-1462 vs. 317-526-1471) without explanation of which to use
No client testimonials or reviews visible on the website
Claims of 'thousands of cases' experience lack independent verification
best_for: Indianapolis-area residents considering Chapter 7 bankruptcy with significant unsecured debt they cannot repay
Individuals with moderate income seeking Chapter 13 reorganization plans over 3-5 years
People facing foreclosure who want legal options analysis beyond bankruptcy
Spanish-speaking consumers in Indiana needing bankruptcy representation in their preferred language
diagnosis: John Steinkamp & Associates is appropriate for Indiana residents seriously considering bankruptcy protection who need legal guidance through Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filing. The main caveat is that bankruptcy should be pursued only after consulting with a bankruptcy attorney about alternatives, as it results in a 7-10 year credit report impact and requires honest disclosure of all assets and liabilities.
services: Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing with payment plan negotiation
Debt collector harassment defense under FDCPA
Credit restoration and credit report dispute services
Automatic stay protection to halt creditor collection activity
Discharged debt credit report removal assistance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 5214 S East St Suite D1, Indianapolis, IN 46227
